heading: Waranga Shores vs Warburton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Waranga Shores, Victoria and Warburton, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Waranga Shores has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Warburton (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Waranga Shores or Warburton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Waranga Shores has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Warburton scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Waranga Shores scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Warburton scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Waranga Shores or Warburton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Waranga Shores has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
